content.thinking:
The question asks if dermatology primarily focuses on the diagnosis and treatment of cancer.

Dermatology is the medical specialty that deals with the skin, hair, nails, and related diseases. While dermatologists do diagnose and treat skin cancers (like melanoma, basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma), that's not the PRIMARY focus of the specialty. Dermatology encompasses a much broader range of conditions including acne, eczema, psoriasis, infections, allergic reactions, cosmetic concerns, and many other skin conditions.

So the answer should be "no" because cancer treatment is not the PRIMARY focus, even though it is part of what dermatologists do.
